comment: Sidecar metadata for .mcp.json. Documents tier and purpose per server without injecting non-standard fields into the MCP config consumed by Claude Code. Authoritative tier table also lives in README.md ("MCP servers" section).

pinning_policy.rationale: Plugin is redistributed; strict pins age out fast. `npx -y` / `uvx` always fetch the latest published version. Trade-off: drift possible between installs.

godot-mcp.purpose: Editor automation: run scenes, query nodes, edit project.godot

credential.where_to_get: Path to a local Godot executable (e.g. /Applications/Godot.app/Contents/MacOS/Godot on macOS, /usr/bin/godot on Linux). Server uses it to extract bundled XML class docs.

godot-docs.note: Server fails fast with `Error: GODOT_PATH environment variable must be set` if the env var is not exported in the user's shell. Earlier versions of this plugin shipped the wrong package name (`godot-docs-mcp`, which does not exist on npm), causing the server to silently fail to start. Fixed in v1.12.1.

context7.purpose: Library / framework docs (gdtoolkit, GUT, Phantom Camera, etc.)

git.purpose: Version control queries (log, diff, blame) without shelling out

meshy.purpose: Meshy AI 3D model generation: text-to-3D, image-to-3D, multi-image-to-3D, retexture, remesh, rigging, animation. Used by `art-director` for 3D-asset scaffolding when the project needs placeholder meshes.
meshy.note: Bundled in `.mcp.json` because the package (`@meshy-ai/meshy-mcp-server`) is canonical and stable. Server only starts if `MESHY_API_KEY` is exported in the user's shell environment; otherwise the `npx` invocation runs with an empty key and the server fails fast at the first API call. Add to `enabledMcpjsonServers` in `settings.local.json` to opt in.
